# Recommended GitHub Repository Topics

Set these topics on the repository's **Settings → Topics** field to maximise discoverability in GitHub Explore, GitHub Search, and external search engines.

## Topics to Add

Copy-paste this list directly into the GitHub Settings → Topics box (space-separated):

```
super-resolution anime image-restoration artifact-removal deep-learning pytorch
upscaling anime-super-resolution image-enhancement jpeg-artifact-removal waifu2x
onnx windows directml unet attention mamba
```

## Why These Topics Matter

| Topic | Why |
|-------|-----|
| `super-resolution` | Primary function; largest relevant topic on GitHub |
| `anime` | Core content domain |
| `image-restoration` | Umbrella term for the task |
| `artifact-removal` | Key differentiation vs. pure upscalers |
| `deep-learning` | Attracts researchers and ML engineers |
| `pytorch` | Attracts developers looking at the training code |
| `upscaling` | Common user-facing search term |
| `anime-super-resolution` | Specific niche term; lower noise |
| `image-enhancement` | Broad umbrella; appears in "Explore" feeds |
| `jpeg-artifact-removal` | Very specific user search intent |
| `waifu2x` | Most-recognised anime SR name; people compare against it |
| `onnx` | Attracts inference/deployment-focused users |
| `windows` | Platform badge; filters for Windows-only users |
| `directml` | Attracts Windows ML / hardware-agnostic GPU users |
| `unet` | Architecture keyword for researchers |
| `attention` | Architecture keyword |
| `mamba` | Emerging SSM architecture; active research community |

## Other Discoverability Actions (Must Be Done in GitHub Settings UI)

1. **Repository Description** — Set a one-liner in Settings → General:
   > *Free 4× anime illustration super-resolution & artifact removal for Windows. Targets real-world internet compression (Pixiv/X/Facebook multi-upload chains).*

2. **Social Preview Image** — Upload a 1280×640 image in Settings → Social preview.
   A before/after side-by-side comparison works very well here.

3. **Website URL** — If you later create a Ko-fi page or demo page, set it as the repository URL in Settings → General.

4. **Pin the repository** on your GitHub profile so it appears front-and-center.
